seoTitle: File Jointly in TurboTax as a Married Couple
metaDescription: Learn how to file jointly in TurboTax as a married couple, including how to maximize deductions and avoid common filing mistakes.
intro: Filing jointly often unlocks tax benefits that separate filing doesn't, but the process still requires care to make sure both spouses' information is entered correctly. This guide covers how to file jointly in TurboTax, from setting up the return to maximizing deductions. It's aimed at married couples navigating a joint return, whether for the first time or as a refresher.

steps.heading: Choose the joint filing status
steps.body: When starting your return in TurboTax, select married filing jointly as your filing status.

steps.heading: Enter both spouses' information
steps.body: Input income, deductions, and personal details for both spouses accurately to reflect the household's full tax picture.

steps.heading: Review deductions and credits together
steps.body: Check for deductions and credits available specifically to joint filers that might not apply to separate returns.

steps.heading: Avoid common mistakes
steps.body: Double-check that all income sources for both spouses are included and no forms, like W-2s or 1099s, are missing.

steps.heading: Finalize and file your return
steps.body: Review the completed joint return for accuracy before submitting it electronically through TurboTax.

faq.question: Is filing jointly always better than filing separately?
faq.answer: Not always — most couples benefit from joint filing, but certain situations, like high medical expenses, may favor separate filing.

faq.question: Do both spouses need to be present to file jointly in TurboTax?
faq.answer: No, one spouse can enter both sets of information as long as they have the necessary documents and consent.

faq.question: What common mistakes happen when filing jointly?
faq.answer: Missing a spouse's income source or overlooking joint-specific deductions are common errors to watch for.

faq.question: Can TurboTax help maximize deductions for joint filers?
faq.answer: Yes, TurboTax reviews entered information to suggest deductions and credits relevant to your joint filing status.
